narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because DXY at 98.12 falling, USD CSM at zero, and geopolitical de-escalation removing safe-haven demand simultaneously
2. AUD and NZD outperformance because CSM scores of 93 and 100 respectively on H4, copper up 2.31% validating commodity currency bid, and risk-on regime accelerating
3. JPY cross selling because USDJPY, EURJPY, GBPJPY, CADJPY all showing H4 sell signals with ADX confirming, driven by yen intervention threat and safe-haven unwind into risk-on

PRICE CONFIRMS

NZDUSD is the cleanest expression of the dominant theme, carrying a strong buy signal of plus 6 on H4 with NZD CSM accelerating hardest at 100 versus 65 on D1, indicating momentum building rather than exhausting. AUDUSD confirms via its 0.91 correlation with NZDUSD and copper price action, and the tight 0.94 correlation between EURUSD and GBPUSD confirms broad USD selling rather than pair-specific idiosyncratic flows. JPY crosses are lagging the USD selling narrative as yen intervention risk creates a ceiling, meaning USDJPY is not the cleanest vehicle despite the directional USD bias aligning.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed with internal conflict on gold. VIX at 17.13 falling 1.44 percent and S&P 500 up 0.81 percent support risk-on, but gold surging 2.56 percent to 4672 alongside silver up 4.42 percent introduces a genuine macro fear signal that prevents a clean risk-on classification at full conviction.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on regime: gold and silver surging aggressively while equities rise and VIX falls is contradictory and suggests either inflation repricing or a latent macro fear bid running parallel to the equity rally
2. EUR CSM divergence versus EURUSD signal weakness: EUR H4 CSM is accelerating to 82 versus a D1 of only 6, yet EUR ranks as one of the weakest currencies overall, creating ambiguity about whether EUR is genuinely recovering or simply benefiting from USD selling without its own fundamental support

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: NZDUSD up - NZD CSM at 100, USD at zero, H4 strong buy signal, copper confirming - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: AUDUSD up - AUD CSM 93, risk-on confirmed, commodity tailwind from copper aligning - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: USDJPY - yen intervention risk creates asymmetric stop-out threat in either direction
Watch: DXY breaking and holding below 98.00 confirms primary trade and validates the D1 transition from mixed to full risk-on

themes.theme: Middle East de-escalation and Iran peace hopes
Risk-on sentiment driving equities and cyclical currencies
Japanese Yen intervention and safe-haven support
Dollar weakness amid improved market optimism
Central bank divergence - Norges Bank hawkish stance
EUR consolidation near key technical levels

key_observation: Risk-on environment dominates following Middle East de-escalation signals and Iran peace hopes. USD weakens broadly as safe-haven flows reverse. Japanese authorities conduct suspected intervention supporting JPY. Equities surge to record highs on tech strength. Gold and commodities under pressure as geopolitical premium unwinds.
watch: EUR/USD range stability, AUD/USD momentum toward 0.7200s, further JPY intervention signals, US-Iran negotiation developments, equity market continuation, DXY support below 98.50, gold holding above 4650
